questions.stem: An object starts at 5 m/s and accelerates uniformly at 3 m/s^2 for 5 s. What is its final velocity?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: For motion with constant acceleration, which equation gives the final velocity from the initial velocity, acceleration, and time?
hints.rationale: With constant acceleration, final velocity equals initial velocity plus acceleration times time.

hints.prompt: Which substitution correctly applies v = u + a t to u = 5 m/s, a = 3 m/s^2, t = 5 s?
hints.rationale: Substitute the initial velocity, then add the product of acceleration and time.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from computing only a*t and forgetting to add the initial velocity. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 15 m/s is just (3)(5), ignoring the 5 m/s initial velocity, so it is a trap rather than the answer.

questions.stem: A current of 5 A flows through a 3 Ω resistor. What is the voltage across it?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: V = I R = (5)(3) = 15 V.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: Which form of Ohm's law relates voltage to current and resistance?
hints.rationale: Ohm's law states voltage equals current times resistance.

hints.prompt: Which substitution correctly applies V = I R to I = 5 A and R = 3 ohm?
hints.rationale: Multiply the current by the resistance.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from dividing current by resistance (5/3) instead of multiplying. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 1.67 V is 5/3, the result of dividing instead of multiplying, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: A wave has frequency 256 Hz and wavelength 2 m. What is its wave speed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: Which relationship connects wave speed to frequency and wavelength?
hints.rationale: Wave speed equals frequency multiplied by wavelength.

hints.prompt: Which substitution correctly applies v = f * lambda to f = 256 Hz and lambda = 2 m?
hints.rationale: Multiply the frequency by the wavelength.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from adding f and lambda instead of multiplying. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 258 m/s is about 256 + 2, the result of adding instead of multiplying, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of CO2?
questions.explanation: CO2 adopts a linear geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: In VSEPR theory, what primarily determines a molecule's geometry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the physical color of the compound

choices.label: B
choices.text: the total number of electrons in the whole molecule

choices.label: C
choices.text: the molar mass of the molecule

choices.label: D
choices.text: the number of electron domains (bonding pairs and lone pairs) around the central atom

hints.rationale: VSEPR geometry is set by the arrangement of electron domains around the central atom.

hints.prompt: How many electron domains surround the central carbon in CO2 (two double bonds, no lone pairs on C)?
hints.rationale: Each double bond counts as one domain, so carbon has two domains and no lone pairs.

hints.prompt: Which listed geometry is the trap for wrongly assuming the central atom has a lone pair (as in SO2)?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: Bent is the trap for assuming a lone pair on carbon, which CO2 does not have.

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of H2O? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in H2O gives a molar mass of about 18 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: How is the molar mass of a compound determined?

choices.label: A
choices.text: by using the atomic mass of the heaviest atom only

choices.label: B
choices.text: by multiplying the number of atoms by Avogadro's number

choices.label: C
choices.text: by averaging the atomic masses of the atoms

choices.label: D
choices.text: by summing the atomic masses of every atom in its formula

hints.rationale: Molar mass is the sum of the atomic masses of all atoms in the formula.

hints.prompt: Which sum gives the molar mass of H2O using H = 1 and O = 16?
hints.rationale: Water has two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, so add 2(1) and 16.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from counting only the oxygen and ignoring the hydrogens. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 16 g/mol uses only oxygen's mass, ignoring both hydrogen atoms, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: What is the molarity of a solution with 6 mol of solute in 1 L of solution?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: How is molarity defined?

choices.label: A
choices.text: grams of solute divided by liters of solution

choices.label: B
choices.text: liters of solution divided by moles of solute

choices.label: C
choices.text: moles of solute multiplied by liters of solution

choices.label: D
choices.text: moles of solute divided by liters of solution

hints.rationale: Molarity is moles of solute per liter of solution.

hints.prompt: Which substitution correctly applies M = moles / liters to 6 mol in 1 L?
hints.rationale: Divide the moles of solute by the volume in liters.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from dividing liters by moles (1/6) instead of moles by liters. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 0.167 M is 1/6, the inverted ratio, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: How much heat is needed to raise 50 g of water by 1 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

hints.prompt: Which equation gives the heat needed to change a substance's temperature?
hints.rationale: Heat equals mass times specific heat times temperature change.

hints.prompt: Which substitution correctly applies q = m c (delta T) to m = 50 g, c = 4.18, delta T = 1?
hints.rationale: Multiply mass, specific heat, and temperature change together.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from using only c times delta T and ignoring the mass. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 4.18 J is just c times delta T, leaving out the 50 g mass, so it is a trap.

questions.stem: A system absorbs 1000 J of heat and does 200 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 1000 - 200 = 800 J.

hints.prompt: For a system that absorbs heat Q and does work W on the surroundings, what is the first law of thermodynamics?
hints.rationale: Internal energy change equals heat added minus work done by the system.

hints.prompt: Which substitution correctly applies (delta U) = Q - W to Q = 1000 J and W = 200 J?
hints.rationale: Subtract the work done from the heat absorbed.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from adding the work instead of subtracting it. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 1200 J is 1000 + 200, the result of adding the work, so it is a trap.

hints.prompt: One wrong choice comes from multiplying the quantities together instead of dividing, giving an enormous value. Which choice is that trap?

choices.label: D
choices.text: None of these - no listed choice matches that mistake

hints.rationale: 2.09 x 10^6 comes from multiplying rather than dividing, so it is a trap.
